strategy: Bull Call Spread $62.50/$70 Aug 2026 — copper demand thesis with defined risk given significant insider selling

wheelStrategy.wheelFlag: INSIDER SELLING WARNING: Chairman Richard Adkerson sold 400,991 shares (~$25.4M) and 9 total insider sales occurred in the past 6 months with 0 purchases. Wheel strategy is technically eligible but insider selling is a meaningful directional headwind — use reduced position sizes (1 contract max) and tighter stops.
wheelStrategy.vixNote: VIX elevated ~20+; commodity stocks carry elevated volatility — FCX options premiums are attractive. However, insider selling is a risk flag that warrants caution on large CSP positions.
wheelStrategy.bollingerEntry: Lower Bollinger Band estimated ~$54–$56. Current price $60.83 is above the lower band — wait for a pullback toward $55–56 before initiating CSP. Do not chase at current levels given insider selling.

csp.exitRule: Close at 50% premium captured (target $0.93–1.05). Hard stop before July 16 (5 days before est. July 21 earnings).
csp.note: The $55 strike is 9.6% below current price — a meaningful buffer given the insider selling risk. Only enter on a pullback toward lower Bollinger Band.

coveredCall.exitRule: Close at 50% premium captured. Do not hold through earnings.
coveredCall.note: If assigned at $55 CSP, sell $60 CC to reduce cost basis to ~$53. Continue wheel only if insider selling abates.

expiryComparison.iv: ~42% (commodity cycle volatility; elevated post-Grasberg disruption news)

exitRules.entryTrigger: Enter spread when copper spot holds above $4.50/lb and stock confirms support at $59.00. Do not enter into momentum down days — wait for stabilization.
exitRules.profitExit: Close Contract 1 when spread reaches 50% of max gain (~$375 on $62.50/$70). Let Contract 2 ride to full $70 target if stock momentum supports it.
exitRules.timeStop: If stock has not moved above $65 by June 15 (50% of DTE), close the spread. Theta on the long leg accelerates into the final 30 days.
exitRules.lossStop: Close at 40–50% loss on net debit paid (close at ~$1.15–1.30 loss on $2.30 net paid). Insider selling makes averaging down particularly risky.
exitRules.earningsHardStop: Close ALL positions by July 16, 2026 — 5 days before estimated July 21 earnings. Copper stocks are extremely volatile into quarterly results. No exceptions.

options.flatScenario: Stock stays at $60.83 — spread expires near worthless. Loss: ~$230 per spread.
options.atTarget: Stock at $70.00 by July 16 close — spread worth ~$6.50–7.00. Gain: ~$420–470 per spread (~180% return).
options.bestFor: Optimal — cheapest premium for the identical close-deadline window.

options.flatScenario: Same close deadline — paying $0.10 extra per spread for 35 days of post-earnings time that is unusable.
options.atTarget: Identical outcome at July 16 close. Marginal extra cost, no extra benefit.
options.bestFor: Only if earnings is pushed past July — provides insurance against date slippage.

options.flatScenario: Paying $0.20 extra per spread for time entirely cut off by earnings hard stop.
options.atTarget: Same close-deadline outcome. Aug strictly cheaper for identical risk exposure.
options.bestFor: Not recommended — overkill. Thesis resolves before Aug expiry anyway.

recommendation.title: Bull Call Spread — $62.50/$70 Aug 15, 2026
recommendation.type: Bull Call Spread (IV ~42% — short $70 leg saves ~$2.50 vs. single call; spread strongly preferred and limits downside given insider selling)
recommendation.entryNote: IV at ~42% makes the $70 short leg save a meaningful $2.50 per contract — spread structure is clearly superior to a single call. More importantly, the 9 insider sales and 0 purchases over 6 months (including the Chairman's $25.4M sale) demand defined-risk positioning. Max loss is $230/spread vs. $480/contract on a naked call. Copper macro thesis is intact: AI data centers and energy transition drive structural demand. Enter only after confirming copper spot >$4.50/lb and stock holds $59 support.

exitPlan.label: Contract 1
exitPlan.optionGain: ~$200–230 per spread (close at 50% of max gain)

companyOverview: Freeport-McMoRan is the world's largest publicly traded copper company, operating major mines in the US (Arizona, New Mexico), South America (Peru, Chile), and Indonesia (Grasberg). Q1 2026 revenue of $6.23B beat estimates by 9%, driven by elevated copper prices and operational improvements. The structural bull case is clear: copper is essential for AI data centers (power infrastructure), EV batteries, and renewable energy. However, two significant headwinds complicate the near-term picture: (1) the 2025 Grasberg mine mud-rush fatality and subsequent restart ramp creates execution risk, and (2) coordinated insider selling of $30M+ in the past six months signals insiders see limited near-term upside. Long-term copper demand is not in question — the question is timing and Grasberg execution.

financials.note: Largest publicly traded pure-play copper company
UBS $74, JPMorgan bullish; MS downgraded to Equal Weight $66

insiderFlow.summary: Heavy and sustained insider selling over 6 months with zero purchases — the single most important risk signal for this trade.

transactions.note: Chairman sold 401K shares across multiple transactions. No corresponding purchase. Most significant insider signal.

transactions.insider: Douglas N. Currault II (EVP & General Counsel)
transactions.note: EVP legal counsel sale. Combined with Chairman sale: coordinated distribution.

insiderFlow.signal: BEARISH — 9 insider sales, 0 purchases over 6 months totaling $30M+. Insiders are reducing exposure at prices ~$63–65, just above current $60.83. Use defined-risk spread structure only.

catalysts: Copper structural demand: AI data center buildout requires massive copper wiring — each MW of data center capacity uses 10–15 tons of copper
Energy transition: EV batteries, solar panels, and wind turbines all copper-intensive; long-term demand floor rising
Q1 2026 revenue beat 9% above estimates ($6.23B vs $5.73B) — operational execution improving
UBS raised target to $74 on copper macro optimism; JPMorgan remains bullish
Grasberg restart ramp scheduled for 2026 — successful execution would unlock significant volume upside
$0.15 quarterly dividend (base + variable component) provides some downside cushion
risks: Sustained insider selling: 9 sales, 0 purchases, $30M+ in 6 months — insiders exiting near current prices is a serious red flag
Grasberg mine disruption: 2025 fatal mud-rush; restart ramp execution risk; Morgan Stanley downgraded on production concerns
Copper price sensitivity: a macro slowdown or China demand weakness would crush FCX earnings quickly
Morgan Stanley downgraded to Equal Weight with $66 target — institutional conviction is splitting
Stock down 7.4% in the past week; near-term momentum is negative
Tariff risk: US trade policy uncertainty could impact global copper trade flows and pricing
dilution: FCX is a mature mining corporation with a stable share count. No significant dilution risk from equity issuance. The company returns capital through dividends (base + variable) rather than buybacks. Debt levels are manageable relative to EBITDA.

strikeTable.atTarget: +$240 at $70 — poor leverage close to target
Barely ITM at analyst target — low probability payoff
